 {"id":"bd-e1eo","title":"Epic: Comprehensive Test Coverage Without Mocks","description":"# Epic: Comprehensive Test Coverage Without Mocks

## Overview
This epic establishes complete test coverage for the repo_updater (ru) project using real implementations instead of mocks. The goal is 90%+ function coverage with detailed logging and full E2E integration tests.

## Current State Analysis
- **Total functions in ru**: 316
- **Functions with unit tests**: 82 (~26%)
- **Functions using mocks**: Most existing tests use function overrides as mocks
- **E2E coverage**: Partial, inconsistent logging

## Target State
- **Unit test coverage**: 90%+ without mocks (real function calls)
- **E2E test coverage**: All major workflows with structured JSON logging
- **Test isolation**: Each test runs in isolated temp directory
- **Logging**: Machine-readable JSON logs with timing, context, and results

## Sub-Epics (5 total)

### 1. Test Framework Enhancement (bd-wrfp)
Enhance test_framework.sh with JSON logging, coverage tracking, and parallel execution.

### 2. Core Functions Unit Tests (bd-fudb)
Unit tests for: URL parsing, git operations, configuration, repo list management, worktree helpers, JSON utilities.

### 3. Review Feature Unit Tests (bd-c3vu)
Unit tests for the review feature (bd-4bmq): state management, locking, discovery, validation, quality gates, gh_actions, metrics.

### 4. Driver Function Tests (bd-68rr)
Unit tests for: local driver, driver interface, rate limit governor.

### 5. E2E Integration Tests (bd-6crg)
Complete workflows: sync, review, clone, worktree, config, error handling. All with comprehensive JSON logging.

## Dependencies
All sub-epics depend on this parent epic. Within E2E tests, the logging infrastructure (bd-g7gw) must be completed first.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] bd stats shows 90%+ function coverage
- [ ] All tests pass without mocks
- [ ] JSON logs capture full audit trail
- [ ] CI pipeline integrates all tests
- [ ] Test execution time < 5 minutes total","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:52:18.285415667-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:30.999144851-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:30.999144851-05:00","close_reason":"Epic planning complete: Created 5 sub-epics and 27 tasks with full dependency structure. Test coverage target: 90%+ without mocks. Implementation work tracked in sub-epics bd-wrfp, bd-fudb, bd-c3vu, bd-68rr, bd-6crg."}

 {"id":"bd-eta6","title":"[EPIC] Parallel Processing & Work Queue","description":"# Parallel Processing & Work Queue

## Purpose
Process multiple repositories concurrently using work-stealing pattern with atomic operations.

## Design Principles
- Reuse ru's existing run_parallel_sync() pattern
- No semaphores, no IPC - just atomic directory operations
- Each repo gets unique session name to avoid collisions
- Work-stealing: fast workers get more repos

## Work Queue Pattern

1. Create work queue (temp file with repo specs)
2. Create lock directories for coordination
3. Spawn N workers as background processes
4. Each worker loops:
   a. Acquire queue lock
   b. Pop first repo from queue
   c. Release queue lock
   d. Process repo (no I/O to shared files during processing)
   e. Acquire results lock
   f. Append result to results file
   g. Release results lock
   h. Update progress counter (with lock)
5. Wait for all workers
6. Aggregate results

## Lock Points
| Lock | Purpose | Timeout |
|------|---------|---------|
| queue.lock | Atomic dequeue from work queue | 30s |
| results.lock | Atomic append to results file | 30s |
| state.lock | Atomic state file updates | 10s |
| backoff.lock | Global rate limit coordination | 10s |

## Session Naming
ru_sweep_{repo_name_sanitized}_{pid}_{worker_index}
Example: ru_sweep_mcp_agent_mail_12345_0

## Global Rate Limit Backoff
When rate limited, set global pause that all workers respect:
- Shared backoff state file
- Workers check before starting new repo
- Exponential backoff with jitter (±25%)
- Max delay: 10 minutes

## Portable Locking
Uses mkdir (atomic on POSIX) instead of flock:
- dir_lock_acquire() - blocking with timeout
- dir_lock_release() - idempotent cleanup

## ntm Session Serialization
CRITICAL: ntm robot commands are sequential per session.
DO NOT call --robot-send while --robot-wait is running on same session.
Each repo has its own session, so no cross-repo serialization needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:46:21.821797346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T00:31:41.998456089-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T00:31:41.998456089-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented - agent-sweep command and parallel processing working with comprehensive tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eta6","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.131315272-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-eta6","depends_on_id":"bd-kvu5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.565238722-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
